title: sorbus aria f. aurea
intro: Sorbus aria f. aurea, also known as the European whitebeam, is a deciduous tree or shrub in the rose family (Rosaceae), characterized by its golden or yellow foliage. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, such as salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. Consuming Sorbus aria f. aurea has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as having antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Sorbus aria f. aurea include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
